free_text: Institut fuer Chemie of the Kernforschungs-
anlage Juelich GmbH, Institut I - Nuclearchemie.
Measuring
technique, error estimation and correction method.
See for HPLC-method.
Data
analysis method.
See for
method of corrections.
.Half-life for isotopes taken from Nuclear Data Sheets,
ORNL,USA by the author.
Measurement of (n,p)reaction cross sections at
14.7 MeV using high-pressure liquid chromatography
in the region of rare earths
Neutron Generator DYNAGEN.
Fast pneumatic tube system for short lived isotopes.
Deuteron-tritium.
14.7 +- 0.3 MeV neutrons.  Irradiation up to 6 hr at
neutron flux densities of (1 - 2) x 10**9 /cm2/sec.
Contribution of thermal neutrons less 0.1%.
.High-purity rare earth oxides (> 99.9%) were sealed in
thin polyethylene foils and sandwiched between two
layers of the flux monitor material (Al or As2O3)
Activation and identification of the radio-
activ reaction products.
Wherever necessary the technique of high-
pressure liquid chromatography (HPLC) was employed
for chemical separation. For details see REL-REF.
.Each measured value is based on at least three
independent measurements.
3 GeLi-spectrometers were used-
a well-type 65 cm3,
a Co-axial 28 cm3 for longer-lived products,
a small one with an active area of 2 cm2 with a thin
beryllium window.
.The detectors were on-line with a 4000-channel
intertechnique DIDAC or a 16K nuclear data system
4420 analyzer.
With sigma=121+-6mb.
With sigma=970+-80mb.
.For counting efficiency, geometry, absorption.
The maximum error is given, it includes the
systematic errors such as the uncertainty in neutron
flux determination, the uncertainty in the relative
photopeak efficiency of the detectors, and the
uncertainty in the evaluation of the Compton back-
ground, particularly in the low energy region.
Combining all individual errors in quadrature.
Errors due to uncertainties in decay schemes are not
included.
.Cross section trends were investigated and described.
Tbl.1 from RRL 25,335.
Corrected data from the Tbl.1 are
presented in 20933 (see Subents 5, 8, 9, 10, 12).

from Tbl.1 of RRL 25,335; HL deleted from DATA section
(value moved to DECAY-DATA) in Subents 2 - 15.
Refs. NP/A,224,319 and JRC,30,35 moved to REL-REF.

See REL-REF). MONIT info was added to COMMON. Subents
016-020 were added (data from Tbl.1 of RRL 25,335).
